channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Knew About Gravity. But I Hadn't Thought About the Equation Behind It This Way
item.description: I didn't "learn" about gravity recently. Like everyone else, I've known the basic idea for years. Objects fall, masses attract each other, and names like Newton or Einstein are so familiar that...

item.title: I Wrote a React Book So You Don't Have to Learn These Lessons the Hard Way
item.description: A few months ago I attended a React conference and realized something uncomfortable: I'd been writing React for years and I was doing it wrong. Not "broken" wrong — my code worked. But it was slow...

item.title: I let Claude Code run marketing workflows for brands
item.description: I got tired of context-switching between Figma, Buffer, Instagram, analytics, random downloads folders, and whatever tab I had open for reference images. The problem was not "marketing is hard" in...

item.title: The Skill That Helps Me Do Code Review
item.description: I was complaining. Again. Code review was eating my time, and the problem was only getting worse. We were shipping more features faster, which meant more code, much of it in domains I don't know...

item.title: npm package commitment scores: zod has 139M weekly downloads and one maintainer
item.description: Supply chain attacks are not a novel threat. But there's a pattern in the data that rarely gets called out directly: the most-downloaded npm packages are often maintained by a single person. High...

item.title: Valid JSON, broken discovery: the UCP checks that should fail your build
item.description: If your /.well-known/ucp file is valid JSON but Google AI Mode or other UCP agents still cannot use it, you do not have a formatting problem. You have a discovery problem. That distinction...

item.title: I guess we now have Sprite Animations
item.description: A while back I posted something about wgpu, and making a game engine. I've since worked on it a bit more, and now we somehow have Animated Sprites! 🎉 Yay! Here’s how it works: I loaded a...

item.title: Zero-Cost API Infrastructure: Running a DaaS Business on an Idle Server
item.description: I Had a Server Doing Nothing 22 Hours a Day I run quantitative trading bots — one for the Korean stock market, one for the US market, one for crypto. They sit on AWS and GCP, watching for signals,...

item.title: The Real Ceiling in Claude Code's Memory System (It’s Not the 200-Line Cap)
item.description: Someone published the full Claude Code source to the internet last week. 512,000 lines of TypeScript across 1,916 files. Like everyone else, we went straight for the memory system. But unlike the...

item.title: XYRONIX
item.description: 🌌 X Y R O N I X // NEURAL CONCEPTUALIZATION MATRIX // v1.0.0 // "Pure, decentralized creation space. Infinite boundaries. Zero latency." XYRONIX is a high-performance, infinite-plane rendering...

item.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL...

item.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
item.description: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS...

item.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
item.description: In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she'd never uploaded them to Spotify,...

item.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
item.description: It’s about to become more expensive for Claude Code subscribers to use Anthropic’s coding assistant with OpenClaw and other third-party tools.

item.title: Buy two Nintendo Switch games, get $30 off at Target
item.description: Target is offering a great deal to some Target Circle members that knocks $30 off the cost of two Nintendo Switch and Switch 2 games. The sale is happening for the rest of the day, expiring at...

item.title: My dream pair of AR gaming glasses needs to have these nine features
item.description: I’ve spent months in the lab testing the latest AR glasses from Xreal and Viture. By “lab,” I mean cozied up on my couch each night, playing my Steam Deck or Nintendo Switch 2 on a huge, virtual...

item.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
item.description: "This looks like AI." It's a phrase I dread seeing as a writer who dabbles in illustration and amateur photography. In a world where generative AI technology is increasingly adept at mimicking the...

item.title: Super Meat Boy 3D makes suffering fun
item.description: The original Super Meat Boy is one of the best-known indie games of all time. Released in 2010, it's a brutally difficult 2D platformer, but so fun to play: The short levels almost feel like...

item.title: The app for tracking TV, movies, podcasts, and everything
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 122, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, go 'Zona, and also you can read all the old editions at the...

item.title: Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
item.description: Plus: The FBI says a recent hack of its wiretap tools poses a national security risk, attackers stole Cisco source code as part of an ongoing supply chain hacking spree, and more.

item.title: Anker’s Nebula P1 projector is the portable sound king
item.description: The Soundcore Nebula P1 from Anker isn't the most portable Google TV projector I've ever reviewed, nor is it the brightest. It doesn't even have a built-in battery. Instead it's a decent video...

item.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
item.description: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and...

item.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
item.description: Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude...

item.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jurassic Fish Chokes on Squid
item.description: Here’s a fossil of a 150-million year old fish that choked to death on a belemnite rostrum: the hard, internal shell of an extinct, squid-like animal. Original paper. As usual, you can also use...

item.title: NASA did eventually solve Artemis II’s Outlook glitch
item.description: On Thursday, during Artemis II's journey to the Moon, commander Reid Wiseman ran into a tech issue some of us back on Earth can relate to: Microsoft Outlook wasn't working. In a conversation...

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

item.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
item.description: The viral AI agentic tool let attackers silently gain admin unauthenticated access.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
item.description: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.
